commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch removes the recursive display-change status-item recovery retry loop, keeps a synchronous macOS 26 guidance re-check after the last recreation, updates focused menu-bar tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. the linked issue and PR body give a concrete path: hide CodexBar in a menu-bar manager, trigger a display change, and inspect Control Center layout plus the recovery log. I did not run the macOS/Thaw scenario in this read-only Linux review, but current main source clearly contains the recursive recovery path being reported.
commands.review_followup: Automation can fix the stale changelog placement; the compatibility tradeoff still needs maintainer review before merge.

target.title: fix: stop retrying display-change recovery to avoid corrupting menu bar managers
target.body: ## Summary - Addresses #1175 - Remove the recursive `schedulePostScreenChangeRecoveryVerification` retry loop from `verifyScreenChangeRecoveryIfNeeded` - Preserve the macOS 26 Allow-in-Menu-Bar guidance via a synchronous re-check immediately after the single recreation ## Problem The retry loop introduced in c305858c called `recreateStatusItemsForVisibilityRecovery()` up to four times per display event. After each recreation, a menu bar manager (Bartender, Ice, Hidden Bar) can temporarily leave the new `NSStatusItem` without a window while re-registering it — making it appear blocked and triggering another recreation cycle. Each cycle is visible to Control Center and menu bar managers as a brand-new item, corrupting Control Center layout and causing managers to re-expose the previously hidden icon. ## Why a synchronous re-check is safe for guidance AppKit materialises the status item button and window synchronously before returning from `recreateStatusItemsForVisibilityRecovery`. Only a system-level block (e.g. the macOS Tahoe allow-list) leaves the item blocked at that point — which is exactly the case where the guidance dialog is useful. A menu bar manager's transient re-registration state resolves in milliseconds and does not survive the synchronous boundary. ## Test plan - [x] Run `./Scripts/compile_and_run.sh` - [x] Hide CodexBar via Ice or another menu bar manager - [x] Trigger a display change (System Settings → Displays → change resolution and revert) - [x] Confirm Control Center is not corrupted and the manager does not re-expose CodexBar's icon - [x] Confirm `"recreating status items again"` appears at most once per display event: ``` log stream --process CodexBar --level debug | grep -i "recreating status items" ``` Screenshots: After: Before: **Notes**: We found this issue while doing testing at Thaw. It breaks and corrupts the control center files, but update 0.29.1 has more side effects like the dock not working and the menu bar not being restored at all after deleting the files.
target.author: diazdesandi

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
